_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 3-4 Seizures (Revised 03/03/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured. For patients < 14 years refer to Pediatric Seizure protocol. Source: NAEMSO Clinical Guidelines.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Treatment Protocol Section 3-4: Seizures — MDHHS Approved 3/3/25, Reviewed 2025. Source: NAEMSO Clinical Guidelines.
purpose: For patients < 14 years refer to Pediatric Seizure-Treatment Protocol.

sections.title: Actively Seizing
points: IF PATIENT IS ACTIVELY SEIZING: protect patient from injury; do not force anything between teeth
Pregnant women 20 weeks gestation up to 6 weeks post birth WITHOUT a seizure disorder history: treat as eclampsia — see magnesium sulfate administration below
Administer midazolam 10 mg IM prior to IV start (Specialist skill)
Check blood glucose (may be MFR skill — see Blood Glucose Testing-Procedure Protocol)
If blood glucose < 60 mg/dL or hypoglycemia is suspected: administer dextrose 25 gm IV per Dextrose-Medication Protocol (Specialist skill)

If no IV access, per MCA selection: administer glucagon 1 mg IM (injectable formulation ONLY) or 3 mg IN (intranasal formulation ONLY, if available per MCA selection) — may be EMT skill per MCA selection
NOTE: Only FDA-approved intranasal glucagon (e.g., Baqsimi) may be given by IN route — injectable glucagon must NOT be given intranasally

If patient is pregnant (eclampsia): administer magnesium sulfate 4 gm over 10 minutes IV/IO until seizure stops — add 4 gm to 100 or 250 mL NS and infuse over approximately 10 minutes; if eclamptic seizure does not stop after magnesium, administer benzodiazepine per below (Specialist skill)
If IV already established and midazolam IM/IN has not been administered: administer midazolam 5 mg IV/IO (Specialist skill)
If seizures persist: repeat midazolam 5 mg IV/IO/IM/IN; contact Medical Control (Specialist skill)

sections.title: Not Actively Seizing
points: IF PATIENT IS NOT ACTIVELY SEIZING:
Altered level of consciousness: refer to Altered Mental Status-Treatment Protocol
Alert: monitor for changes; obtain vascular access (Specialist skill); check blood glucose (may be MFR skill)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may designate blood glucose testing as an MFR skill
MCA must select which provider levels may administer 1 mg glucagon IM (injectable formulation only) when IV access is not available
MCA must select which provider levels may administer 3 mg glucagon IN (FDA-approved intranasal formulation only, e.g., Baqsimi) when IV access is not available

key_drugs.name: Midazolam 10 mg IM (Initial Seizure)
key_drugs.dose: 10 mg IM — administer prior to IV start
key_drugs.indication: Active seizure — first-line treatment before IV access is established
key_drugs.contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to benzodiazepines; use with caution in respiratory depression
key_drugs.notes: Administer IM first while IV access is being established. Monitor airway closely after administration.

key_drugs.name: Midazolam 5 mg IV/IO (With IV Access)
key_drugs.indication: Active seizure when IV access is already established and IM midazolam has not been given
key_drugs.contraindications: Known hypersensitivity; respiratory compromise — have airway support ready
key_drugs.notes: If seizures persist: repeat 5 mg IV/IO/IM/IN and contact Medical Control.

key_drugs.name: Dextrose 25 gm IV
key_drugs.indication: Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL or suspected hypoglycemic seizure
key_drugs.contraindications: Confirmed normo- or hyperglycemia without hypoglycemic cause

key_drugs.name: Glucagon 1 mg IM / 3 mg IN
key_drugs.dose: 1 mg IM (injectable only) or 3 mg IN (FDA-approved IN formulation only)
key_drugs.indication: Hypoglycemic seizure when IV access cannot be established
key_drugs.contraindications: Do NOT use injectable formulation via IN route; pheochromocytoma
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT through Paramedic options available
key_drugs.notes: Only Baqsimi (or equivalent FDA-approved IN glucagon) may be given intranasally.

key_drugs.name: Magnesium Sulfate 4 gm IV/IO (Eclampsia)
key_drugs.dose: 4 gm over 10 minutes IV/IO — add to 100 or 250 mL NS and infuse over approximately 10 minutes
key_drugs.indication: Eclamptic seizure — pregnant woman ≥ 20 weeks gestation to 6 weeks post birth without prior seizure disorder
key_drugs.side_effects: Flushing, hypotension, loss of deep tendon reflexes, respiratory depression at high levels
key_drugs.notes: If eclamptic seizure does not stop after magnesium: proceed to benzodiazepine (midazolam). Monitor respiratory status closely.

deterioration_flags: Status epilepticus (seizure > 5 minutes or multiple seizures without return to baseline) — midazolam IM immediately; IV access; repeat midazolam if persistent; contact Medical Control
Eclamptic seizure (pregnant ≥ 20 wks without seizure history) — magnesium sulfate 4 gm IV/IO (Specialist skill); if fails, midazolam; contact Medical Control
Post-seizure hypoglycemia — blood glucose check; dextrose IV or glucagon if no IV (per MCA selection)
Respiratory depression after benzodiazepine — airway management priority; prepare for advanced airway
scope_notes: Patient protection, scene safety: all levels
Blood glucose testing: all levels — may be MFR skill per MCA selection
Glucagon IM/IN: per MCA selection — EMT through Paramedic options
